Details:
Results from a bench-scale study showed that the addition of hydrogen peroxide resulted in no methomyl removal in deionized water. However, a bench-scale study evaluating the effectiveness of Fenton’s reagent, or hydrogen peroxide in combination with Fe2+, achieved 86 percent removal of the contaminant. This increased removal is due to the formation of hydroxyl radicals during Fenton’s process.
pH of 3 was atypical of drinking water treatment.
